notes: Q2: fed 3657 bytes from pipe-pane raw-ANSI log ../pipe-pane-probe/EVIDENCE/tui.stream.log.

Q2: pipe-pane raw bytes are accepted by the parser (no crash), but the load-bearing `tui_alt_screen` HIGH-confidence rule never fires because pipe-pane strips alt-screen toggles (BF-206 finding: toggleCount=0). No byte-level sanitizer recovers the missing info; the fix is structural — use BF-207's node-pty(tmux attach) source instead of pipe-pane.
Q2: control feed of BF-203 capture-pane .log (836 bytes) — emulator accepted bytes, altScreen=false.
Q3: fed 2402 chars from pipe-pane raw-ANSI log ../pipe-pane-probe/EVIDENCE/tui.stream.log.

Q3 (LOAD-BEARING): existing read_terminal_output sanitizer collapses pipe-pane raw .log to ~0 readable chars (essentially empty). Root cause: pipe-pane emits `
` line terminators; the sanitizer's CR-overwrite heuristic discards everything before the trailing `` on each line. A 1-LOC patch (`cleaned = cleaned.replace(/+
/g, '
')` before the existing CRLF normalize) lifts output to ~649 readable chars containing the expected text content (Claude version, cwd, status tokens).

Q3: input contained 622 UTF-8 box/symbol chars (U+2500-259F + U+2600-27BF) which the printable-ASCII filter drops. Text content survives the fix; UI glyphs do not. To preserve glyphs widen the filter (~5 LOC: add the two Unicode ranges to the keep set).

Q3: BF-203 capture-pane .log control feed (836 bytes) -> 566 readable chars with unmodified production sanitizer (includes "no token"). Confirms the fix only affects pipe-pane raw .log, not normal CRLF streams.
